title: Beyond Gemini: Embracing the Future with Antigravity CLI
text: The world of command-line AI is evolving faster than ever. For years, Gemini CLI has been our faithful companion, turning complex tasks into simple commands. But what if you could do more than just talk to one agent? What if you could orchestrate a symphony of specialized agents working in parallel to solve your most complex problems? Enter Antigravity CLI (agy)—the official successor to Gemini CLI. Are you ready to migrate?

title: Beyond Gemini: Embracing the Future with Antigravity CLI
text: The world of command-line AI is evolving faster than ever. For years, Gemini CLI has been our faithful companion, turning complex tasks into simple commands. But what if you could do more than just talk to one agent? What if you could orchestrate a symphony of specialized agents working in parallel to solve your most complex problems? Enter Antigravity CLI (agy)—the official successor to Gemini CLI. Are you ready to migrate?

title: Beyond Gemini: Embracing the Future with Antigravity CLI
section: Key Highlights: Why Antigravity is a Game Changer
text:
Key Highlights: Why Antigravity is a Game Changer

Parallel Multi-Agent Orchestration: Unlike Gemini’s single-agent approach, Antigravity can spin up multiple subagents simultaneously to tackle different parts of a project at once.
Go-Based Performance: Rewritten in Go, the new CLI offers near-instant startup times and significantly lower resource overhead compared to the Node.js-based Gemini CLI.
Advanced Workflow Commands: New capabilities like /goal for complex task management and /schedule for recurring agentic workflows redefine what’s possible in a terminal.

title: Beyond Gemini: Embracing the Future with Antigravity CLI
section: Deep Dive: The Transition from Gemini to Antigravity
text:
Deep Dive: The Transition from Gemini to Antigravity

1. From Reactive to Orchestrated: The Multi-Agent Power
Gemini CLI operates on a reactive, single-agent model. You ask, it answers. Antigravity CLI introduces Parallel Subagents. Imagine you are building a new feature. With Antigravity, you can use the /goal command:

agy /goal "Implement a new authentication module with tests and documentation"

Behind the scenes, Antigravity spins up three subagents: one to write the code, one to draft the tests, and one to generate documentation. They work in parallel, communicating via a shared context, and deliver the result in a fraction of the time.


2. Speed and Efficiency: The Go Advantage
One of the most immediate changes you’ll notice is speed. Gemini CLI, being Node.js-based, has a slight delay during startup. Antigravity is a compiled Go binary. This means:

Instant Start: No more waiting for the Node runtime to initialize.
Lower Memory Footprint: Better efficiency when running long-lived background tasks.
Simplified Distribution: A single binary that works across systems without worrying about Node versions.



3. A Seamless Migration Path
Google has made the transition as painless as possible. You don’t have to start from scratch.

Importing Plugins: Use agy plugin import gemini to bring your favorite extensions over.
Config Compatibility: Your existing GEMINI.md and AGENTS.md files are fully supported.
The “Doctor” Tool: Run agy doctor to verify your environment and get tips on optimizing your setup for the new platform.

title: Beyond Gemini: Embracing the Future with Antigravity CLI
text:
Conclusion: The Road Ahead
The sunsetting of Gemini CLI on June 18, 2026, isn’t just an end; it’s a beginning. Antigravity CLI represents a shift toward more autonomous, efficient, and powerful AI interactions. By moving to agy, you’re not just changing a command; you’re upgrading your entire development workflow to be more asynchronous and agent-centric. The future of the CLI is here, and it’s weightless.

title: Beyond Gemini: Embracing the Future with Antigravity CLI

title: Master the CLI: A Complete Guide to Google Antigravity (agy)
text: The era of single-tasking AI is over. If you’ve been using the Gemini CLI, you know how powerful a terminal-based agent can be. But have you ever felt the need for speed—or wished your agent could handle multiple tasks at once without breaking a sweat? Meet Antigravity CLI (agy), the high-performance, Go-based successor that turns your terminal into a multi-agent command center. Whether you’re a seasoned developer or just starting, this guide will show you how to harness its full potential.

title: Master the CLI: A Complete Guide to Google Antigravity (agy)
text: The era of single-tasking AI is over. If you’ve been using the Gemini CLI, you know how powerful a terminal-based agent can be. But have you ever felt the need for speed—or wished your agent could handle multiple tasks at once without breaking a sweat? Meet Antigravity CLI (agy), the high-performance, Go-based successor that turns your terminal into a multi-agent command center. Whether you’re a seasoned developer or just starting, this guide will show you how to harness its full potential.

title: Master the CLI: A Complete Guide to Google Antigravity (agy)
section: Key Summary: Why You Should Switch Today
text:
Key Summary: Why You Should Switch Today

Parallel Orchestration: Run complex goals that spin up multiple specialized subagents simultaneously to tackle different parts of a project at once.
Lightning Performance: Built with Go, the new CLI offers near-instant startup times and significantly lower resource overhead.
Seamless Extensibility: Easily create custom tools using “Agent Skills” or connect to local servers via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

title: Master the CLI: A Complete Guide to Google Antigravity (agy)
section: Detailed Explanation: Setting Up and Using Antigravity
text:
Detailed Explanation: Setting Up and Using Antigravity

1. Installation: Getting Started in Seconds
Installing Antigravity is designed to be a one-line process for any operating system. Open your terminal and run the appropriate command:

Windows (PowerShell):
irm https://antigravity.google/cli/install.ps1 | iex
macOS / Linux:
curl -fsSL https://antigravity.google/cli/install.sh | bash
After the script finishes, restart your terminal. You can verify the installation by typing agy --version.



2. Terminal Mastery: Commands and Shortcuts
Once inside the agy environment, you have several powerful ways to interact with the agent and your system: - Run Shell Commands: You don’t need to exit the CLI to run system commands. Prefix any command with ! to execute it directly (e.g., !npm install or !ls -la). - Slash Commands: Use built-in shortcuts like /fork to branch your conversation into a new workspace or /inspect to see active plugins and skills. - Asynchronous Workflows: For long-running tasks, use the /goal command. You can continue asking quick “By The Way” questions using /BTW while the main goal is being processed in the background.


3. Configuration: The New Settings Standard
Antigravity CLI replaces the traditional .env and settings.json manual edits with a more user-friendly interface. To manage your preferences, simply type: > /config This command opens an interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) where you can manage permissions, toggle features, and select your preferred models. Your settings are automatically synchronized and stored in ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json.


4. Custom Tools: Agent Skills and MCP
Extending the CLI is easier than ever. - Agent Skills: These are simple Markdown files that define custom agent behaviors. You can place them in ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/skills/ for global use, or in your project’s .agents/skills/ directory for project-specific commands. - MCP Servers: If you have custom local tools, you can configure them in ~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json to give your agent access to specific databases, APIs, or local services.

title: Master the CLI: A Complete Guide to Google Antigravity (agy)
text:
Conclusion: Elevate Your Development Workflow
Transitioning to Antigravity CLI is more than just a software update; it’s an upgrade to your entire development environment. By leveraging parallel subagents and the speed of a Go-native binary, you can focus more on high-level architecture and less on the minutiae of manual terminal management. The future of AI orchestration is here—it’s fast, multi-threaded, and ready for your next big project.
